description: In this tutorial, the speaker discusses the modeling of resources in a permissionless consensus setting, focusing on external resources like ASICs and memory chips, and introduces the concept of permit oracles for resource allocation. The differences between external and on-chain resources are highlighted, particularly in terms of allocation and confiscation. The tutorial also covers how to model Bitcoin's proof of work and the constraints involved in resource requests.
topic_summary: This video covers the modeling of external resources in permissionless consensus, the introduction of permit oracles, and the differences between external and on-chain resources. It also details how Bitcoin's proof of work can be modeled within this framework.
